content:
# Luke Chapter 22 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Date:** 2026-03-05

This log records every verse where the three translators (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA) diverged in rendering, along with SOPHIA's consensus resolution.

---

## Critical Textual Variant

| Verses | Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Resolution |
|--------|-------|------|-------|--------|-------------------|
| 43-44 | Inclusion/bracketing of angel and bloody sweat | Included with brackets | Included without brackets | Included without brackets | **Included with brackets.** Sinaiticus original hand includes them, but absence from P75 and Vaticanus warrants brackets to signal disputed status. |

---

## Verse-by-Verse Divergences

| Verse | Issue | EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic) | SOPHIA Consensus |
|-------|-------|----------------|----------------|-------------------|------------------|
| 1 | Feast name / verb | "feast of the unleavened-bread was drawing-near, the [one] being-called Pascha" | "feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was drawing near" | "Festival of Unleavened Bread -- called the Passover -- was approaching" | "feast of Unleavened Bread, which is called the Passover, was drawing near" (LOGOS) |
| 3 | Clause structure for "of the twelve" | "being from the number of the twelve" | "being of the number of the twelve" | "who was one of the Twelve" | "who was of the number of the twelve" (blend) |
| 4 | Verb for conferring; verb for betrayal | "spoke-together with"; "hand-over" | "conferred with"; "deliver up" | "discussed with"; "hand over" | "conferred with" (LOGOS); "hand over" (PNEUMA/EMET) |
| 5 | Payment term | "silver" (argyrion literal) | "money" | "money" | "money" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 6 | "favorable-time" vs "opportunity" | "favorable-time" | "opportunity" | "opportunity" | "opportunity" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 7 | "Passover" vs "Passover lamb" | "Pascha [Passover lamb]" | "Passover" | "Passover lamb" | "Passover lamb" (EMET/PNEUMA -- contextually the sacrifice is meant) |
| 8 | Imperative form | "Having gone, prepare" | "Go and prepare" | "Go and prepare" | "Go and prepare" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 9 | "will" vs "want" | "Where do you will" | "Where do you will" | "Where do you want" | "Where do you want" (PNEUMA -- modern English) |
| 10 | Vessel term | "jar of water" | "pitcher of water" | "water jar" | "jar of water" (EMET) |
| 12 | "having-been-furnished" elaboration | "having-been-furnished" | "furnished" | "already furnished and ready" | "furnished" (LOGOS) |
| 15 | Hebraic cognate construction | "With desire I desired" | "With desire I have desired" | "I have longed intensely" | "With desire I have desired" (LOGOS -- preserves Hebraism) |
| 16 | Verb tense/mood | "I will certainly not eat of it until it should be fulfilled" | "I shall not eat it until it be fulfilled" | "I will not eat it again until it finds its fulfillment" | "I shall not eat it until it is fulfilled" (close to LOGOS) |
| 21 | "handing-over" vs "betrays" | "the hand of the [one] handing-over me" | "the hand of the one who delivers me up" | "the hand of my betrayer" | "the hand of the one who betrays me" (blend) |
| 22 | "handed-over" vs "betrayed"; "indeed" | "the Son of Man indeed goes... he is handed-over" | "the Son of Man goes... he is delivered up" | "The Son of Man is going to his fate... betrays him" | "the Son of Man indeed goes... he is betrayed" (EMET's "indeed" + PNEUMA's "betrayed") |
| 24 | "contention" vs "dispute" | "contention" | "dispute" | "dispute" | "dispute" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 27 | "in the midst of you" phrasing | "I am in the midst of you" | "I am in the midst of you" | "here I am among you" | "I am in your midst" (slight variation for flow) |
| 29 | "appoint" vs "grant" | "I appoint to you" | "I appoint to you" | "I am granting you" | "I appoint to you" (EMET/LOGOS -- preserves covenantal verb diatithemai) |
| 31 | Plural "you" + sifting image | "demanded to have you [plural] to sift you as the wheat" | "has demanded to sift you as wheat" | "has demanded to sift all of you like wheat" | "has demanded to sift you all like wheat" (PNEUMA's explicit plural) |
| 32 | "entreated" vs "prayed" | "I entreated concerning you" | "I have prayed for you" | "I have prayed for you" | "I have prayed for you" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 34 | "I say to you" vs "I tell you" | "I say to you" | "I tell you" | "I tell you" | "I tell you" (LOGOS/PNEUMA consensus) |
| 37 | "end" vs "fulfillment" vs "conclusion"; "lawless-ones" vs "lawless" vs "outlaws" | "the [thing] concerning me has [its] end"; "lawless-ones" | "that which concerns me has its fulfillment"; "lawless" | "what is written about me is reaching its conclusion"; "outlaws" | "what concerns me has its fulfillment" (LOGOS); "lawless" (LOGOS) |
| 42 | Adversative conjunction | "but not my will, but yours let-it-be-done" | "nevertheless, not my will but yours be done" | "Yet not my will, but yours be done" | "yet not my will, but yours be done" (PNEUMA's "yet") |
| 43-44 | Brackets; "agony" vs "anguish"; sweat description | Brackets; "an agony"; "drops of blood falling-down upon the earth" | No brackets; "agony"; "drops of blood falling down upon the ground" | No brackets; "anguish"; "his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood" | Brackets; "agony" (EMET/LOGOS); "his sweat became like drops of blood falling down upon the ground" (EMET/LOGOS structure) |
| 45 | "grief" vs "sorrow" | "sleeping from the grief" | "sleeping from sorrow" | "asleep, exhausted by grief" | "sleeping from grief" (EMET/PNEUMA's "grief") |
| 48 | Verb and sentence structure | "with a kiss you hand-over the Son of Man?" | "do you deliver up the Son of Man with a kiss?" | "are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?" | "do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?" (LOGOS structure + PNEUMA's "betray") |
| 51 | "Permit even this" vs "Stop! No more of this!" | "Permit [it] even [to] this" | "Permit even this" | "Stop! No more of this!" | "Permit even this" (EMET/LOGOS -- preserves Greek ambiguity) |
| 52 | "robber" vs "rebel" | "robber" | "robber" | "rebel" | "robber" (EMET/LOGOS consensus) |
| 53 | "the authority of the darkness" vs "when darkness rules" | "the authority of the darkness" | "the authority of darkness" | "the hour when darkness rules" | "the authority of darkness" (LOGOS) |
| 56 | "servant-girl" vs "maidservant" vs "servant girl" | "servant-girl" | "maidservant" | "servant girl" | "servant girl" (PNEUMA -- most natural) |
| 61 | "looked-at" vs "looked upon" vs "looked straight at" | "looked-at Peter" | "looked upon Peter" | "looked straight at Peter" | "looked at Peter" (simplified) |
| 64 | "covered" vs "blindfolded" | "having covered him" | "having blindfolded him" | "They blindfolded him" | "having blindfolded him" (LOGOS/PNEUMA -- contextually accurate) |
| 65 | Word order of "blaspheming" | "many other things, blaspheming, they were saying" | "many other things they were saying against him, blaspheming" | "they hurled all kinds of insults at him" | "many other things they were saying against him, blaspheming" (LOGOS) |
| 66 | "Sanhedrin" vs "council" | "their Sanhedrin [council]" | "their council" | "their Sanhedrin" | "their council" (LOGOS -- English term for Gentile readers) |
| 67 | "Christ" vs "Messiah" | "the Christ" | "the Christ" | "the Messiah" | "the Christ" (EMET/LOGOS -- Greek manuscript warrants Greek title) |
| 70 | Emphasis on "you" | "You yourselves say that I am" | "You say that I am" | "You are the ones saying that I am" | "You yourselves say that I am" (EMET -- preserves emphatic hymeis) |

---

## Summary Statistics

- **Total verses:** 71
- **Verses with divergence requiring adjudication:** 40
- **Verses with full or near-full agreement:** 31
- **Resolutions favoring EMET:** 6
- **Resolutions favoring LOGOS:** 15
- **Resolutions favoring PNEUMA:** 5
- **Resolutions blending two or three translators:** 14
- **Critical textual variants adjudicated:** 1 (vv. 43-44)

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA*
